title: Display a white border around the OWR window for camera tracking
notes:
Going the BepInEx route: write a standalone BepInEx plugin that draws a white frame via Unity IMGUI (OnGUI + DrawTexture). Render the border inside the game itself — no Wayland positioning, no window-decoration tricks. Plugin code in /home/braino/workspace/sinden/SindenBorder/. Compiled .dll lands in OWR BepInEx/plugins/ alongside the DemulShooter plugin.
Verified: 30px white border visible in-game on next OWR launch after dropping SindenBorder.dll into BepInEx/plugins/.

title: Wire Sinden gun event device into OWR plugin via TCP bridge
notes:
Bridge verified end-to-end: reads /dev/input/event24, transforms ABS coords to OWR window coords, sends raw payload to 127.0.0.1:33610. Confirmed crosshair parked at game-window top-left when bridge sent (0,0). Architecture works. Daemon-driven gun events not yet seen because daemon needs a white border on screen to compute valid aim — that's now the blocker (sinden-wq5).
MILESTONE 2026-05-11: User completed the first OWR training level using the Sinden Lightgun on Linux. Full chain works end-to-end: SindenBorder BepInEx plugin draws border → Sinden Mono daemon tracks via camera → gun emits HID events → bridge grabs and forwards via TCP → DemulShooter plugin updates in-game aim. Remaining: jitter (EMA alpha=0.8 not heavy enough), calibration tuning, second-gun player.

title: Map gun front/rear buttons to OWR Heal and Gun-Switch actions
notes: Verified working: rear-left → 'h' (heal), rear-right → Space (cover), front-right → middle-mouse (weapon switch via DemulShooter ChangeWeapon), pump-action → right-mouse (reload). All confirmed in-game by user.

title: Calibration mode: map gun screen position to OWR window coords
notes:
Bridge supports tunable calibration (--x-min/--x-max/--y-min/--y-max, --y-flip) and EMA smoothing (--smooth) plus outlier rejection (--max-jump, --max-reject-frames). Current good defaults: --x-min 0 --x-max 32767 --y-min 0 --y-max 32767 --smooth 0.5 (no outlier filter needed when actually aiming at screen — outlier filter helped catch false rectangles when gun pointed away from screen). Open follow-ups: tune defaults from gameplay feedback; verify if SindenBorder width=80 (in BepInEx/config/braino.sindenborder.cfg) tracks more reliably than 30.
Calibration verified flawless with: SindenBorder width=150, Sinden daemon MatchOnlyWherePointing=1 + ColourMatchRange=120 (tighter), --x-min 0 --x-max 32767 (direct mapping), --smooth 0.5, --max-jump 0 (outlier filter not needed when gun actually points at screen). Center-of-screen tracking issue fixed by MatchOnlyWherePointing.

title: Add second-gun (player 2) support to bridge
notes: Bridge already grabs both event24 + event26, reads ABS_X/Y + buttons for both guns, and sends Axis_X[2]/Axis_Y[2] in TCP payload. Combined debug log added showing g1+g2 state. OWR's own 2P mode renders both crosshairs when both are sent.

title: Reverse-engineer Sinden serial protocol to drive game-event recoil
notes:
Investigation plan written to docs/sinden-serial-recoil.md. Picks up with: decompile LightgunMono.exe via ilspycmd → identify SerialPort.Write byte sequences for Recoil → cross-check with strace → decide shared-port vs full reimpl → wire game's TcpOutputData.Recoil edge into a serial.write of the recoil command in owr-bridge.py. Long-term unlocks per-weapon strength + no false recoil during cover/menus.
Search confirmed: no public source for Sinden Mono daemon or gun firmware exists. All community Linux/MiSTer projects wrap the closed-source LightgunMono.exe binary. SindenPS (closest open-source project) only reads gun HID input, never writes commands back. Reverse-engineering plan as written in docs/sinden-serial-recoil.md is the only path. No duplicate work.
INVESTIGATION COMPLETE 2026-05-12. Findings written to docs/sinden-serial-protocol.md. Decompiled LightgunMono.exe via monodis (IL); decoded all 8 distinct AA-framed config commands + the DE-framed runtime aim stream. Cross-verified with strace + HID descriptor analysis. Conclusion: there is no fire-recoil command in the daemon's protocol vocabulary; the gun's microcontroller fires the solenoid based on local trigger button state ONLY, configured at boot via 0xA2 AssignRecoil. Game-event-driven recoil would require: (a) brute-force fuzzing unknown command bytes (risky), (b) custom gun firmware (huge), (c) hardware mod with relay-driven solenoid (most realistic), or (d) accept the limitation. Accepting (d) for now — current per-trigger-pull recoil with tunable strength is the entire API Sinden offers. The TcpOutputData stream (already consumed by bridge) can still drive non-solenoid haptics + telemetry features.
close_reason: Investigation complete — game-event-driven recoil not achievable without firmware mod or hardware relay; current tuned per-trigger-pull recoil is the full Sinden API. Documented in docs/sinden-serial-protocol.md.

title: Pick up SindenBorder config reload — currently requires OWR restart to apply width change
Package as systemd user service that autostarts with OWR
Hardware relay for game-event-driven recoil (Path 3 option)
description: If we ever want true per-shot game-driven recoil, the cleanest path is a hardware relay that taps the gun's solenoid wires and is driven by a Pi GPIO from the bridge. See docs/sinden-serial-protocol.md "Architectural conclusion" section. Big lift (open gun chassis, solder, GPIO board) but bypasses Sinden firmware entirely.
